title: Display Technologies Compared: OLED · TFT · ePaper

zones.label: OLED (SSD1306)
zones.summary: Tiny, low-power monochrome screen — true blacks
facts: Module: SSD1306, 0.96 inch; resolution 128 × 64 pixels
Colors: monochrome — white, blue, or yellow/blue split (no color mixing)
Interface: I2C (address 0x3C or 0x3D) or SPI
Power: ~20–40 mW (no backlight — each pixel self-emits)
Contrast: effectively infinite — true black pixels are completely off
Refresh: fast (no refresh delay); viewing angle ~160°
Cost: $2–$5
MicroPython: from ssd1306 import SSD1306_I2C; oled.text('Hi', 0, 0); oled.show()
Best for: low-power sensors, wearables, simple text/icon overlays

zones.label: TFT Color LCD (ILI9341)
zones.summary: Full-color screen for images and games
facts: Module: ILI9341, 2.4 inch; resolution 240 × 320 pixels (QVGA)
Colors: 65,536 (16-bit RGB565); some support 262,144 (18-bit)
Interface: SPI (4-wire: MOSI, SCK, CS, DC + RST, BL)
Power: ~100–300 mW (LED backlight required)
Contrast: ~300:1 typical (IPS panels 700:1+); viewing angle 80° (IPS 160°+)
Refresh: fast (good for video and animation)
MicroPython: ili9341 library; display.fill(color); display.text('Hi', x, y, color)
Best for: games, images, color dashboards, anything needing color graphics

zones.label: ePaper / E-Ink (SSD1680)
zones.summary: Paper-like screen that uses zero power when static
facts: Module: SSD1680, 2.9 inch; resolution 296 × 128 (or 200 × 200 at 1.54 inch)
Colors: black + white; some panels add red as a third color
Interface: SPI + multiple GPIO pins (BUSY, RST, DC, CS)
Power: ~0 mW when static; only draws power during a refresh cycle
Contrast: reflects ambient light like printed paper; viewing angle ~180°
Refresh: slow — 2–5 s for a full refresh; partial refresh ~0.3 s
MicroPython: epaper library; display.text('Hi', x, y); display.display() — then wait
Best for: price tags, name badges, rarely-updated dashboards, battery displays

quiz.question: Which display uses essentially zero power when the image is not changing?
quiz.explanation: ePaper (E-Ink) only draws power during a refresh — once the image is drawn it holds with no power, making it ideal for battery-powered price tags and badges.

quiz.question: Which display is the best choice for showing full-color images and games?
quiz.explanation: A TFT color LCD (ILI9341) shows 65,536 colors at 240×320 and refreshes fast enough for animation, making it the right pick for color graphics and games.

quiz.question: Which display is the smallest and lowest-power, with true black pixels and an I2C interface?
quiz.explanation: The SSD1306 OLED is tiny (0.96 inch), draws only 20–40 mW because each pixel self-emits (true blacks), and connects over simple 2-wire I2C — great for wearables.

quiz.question: Which display has the slowest refresh, taking 2–5 seconds to update fully?
quiz.explanation: ePaper refreshes slowly (2–5 seconds for a full update) because it physically rearranges ink particles. That is the trade-off for its paper-like look and zero static power.

quiz.question: Which display needs an LED backlight and therefore uses the most power?
quiz.explanation: A TFT color LCD requires an LED backlight to be visible, drawing ~100–300 mW — far more than the self-emitting OLED or the static-power-free ePaper.
